name: Eric Friedman (Qingyuan Liang)
headline: Space- and ground-based gravitational-wave detector analysis, simulation, and particle-physics phenomenology.
summary: I am a physics graduate researcher at the University of Chinese Academy of Sciences working on stochastic gravitational-wave backgrounds, time-domain simulations for space-based detectors, ground-based detector analysis, LIGO calibration uncertainty, SPIIR pipeline analysis, and phenomenological probes of beyond-the-Standard-Model scalar sectors.
footer: Local research homepage. Data sources: local project folders, server status snapshots, profile.json, and progress.json.

cv.overview: Graduate researcher in physics with a research focus on gravitational-wave observation and data analysis. My work connects detector-level modeling, Bayesian inference, Fisher-matrix forecasting, pipeline reverse engineering, calibration systematics, and particle-physics interpretation of stochastic gravitational-wave backgrounds. I use Python, Mathematica, MATLAB, Bilby, PyGWB, PyDARM, PyMC, emcee, and GPU-oriented workflows for long-running inference and simulation tasks.

education.institution: University of Chinese Academy of Sciences (UCAS), Beijing
education.note: Research focus: gravitational-wave observation and data analysis; supervised by Prof. Huaike Guo.

education.institution: University of Science and Technology Beijing (USTB), Beijing
education.note: Undergraduate thesis: Electromagnetic waves induced by accelerated electrons.

workHighlights.title: Space-based stochastic-background inference
workHighlights.description: Developed Bayesian and Fisher-analysis workflows for first-order phase-transition stochastic backgrounds in LISA/Taiji-like detectors, including sound-wave spectra, astrophysical foreground/background components, and xSM/CxSM model interpretation.

workHighlights.title: Time-domain detector simulation
workHighlights.description: Implemented and analyzed second-generation TDI time streams, A/E/T and XYZ channel representations, equal-arm versus unequal-arm comparisons, and detector-response effects that are hidden by sensitivity-curve-only studies.

workHighlights.title: LIGO Scientific Collaboration work
workHighlights.description: Participated in LIGO-related work on calibration uncertainty and isotropic stochastic-background analysis using PyGWB, Bilby, and PyDARM, with experience in data-quality monitoring duties.

workHighlights.title: SPIIR online CBC pipeline analysis
workHighlights.description: Reverse engineered the SPIIR post-coherent pipeline from command-line configuration to CUDA postcoh, coherent/null SNR, chi-square statistics, background accumulation, rank/FAP construction, FAR assignment, and single-detector likelihood-ratio extensions.

workHighlights.title: Galactic binary foreground simulation
workHighlights.description: Designed a detector-response-first workflow for injecting Galactic double-white-dwarf signals into space-based detector data, including fast SNR screening, resolved/unresolved splitting, grouped GPU response caches, and final TDI A/E/T products.

workHighlights.title: Scalar-sector cosmology and collider complementarity
workHighlights.description: Worked on Bayesian interpretation of scalar-sector phase transitions, including complex singlet and triplet scalar models, Higgs self-coupling deviations, gravitational-wave observables, and collider-facing parameter scans.

currentProjects.title: Time-domain simulations for space-based gravitational-wave detectors
currentProjects.description: This project builds a Bayesian time-domain framework for stochastic gravitational-wave background searches with Taiji/LISA-like interferometers. It starts from second-generation TDI data streams with time-dependent arm lengths, constructs A/E/T and XYZ spectral products, compares equal-arm and unequal-arm response/noise models, and performs nested-sampling inference for astrophysical and first-order phase-transition backgrounds.
currentProjects.role: I work on the detector-response modeling, time-domain data construction, likelihood organization, figure generation, posterior comparison, and manuscript-level scientific interpretation.
currentProjects.progress: The main manuscript is mature: the current local paper contains a full abstract, introduction, detector-model sections, astrophysical-background validation, sound-wave Bayesian analysis, xSM/CxSM projection, and conclusion. The server side also contains multirealization XYZ full-covariance PE outputs and recent environment/reproducibility updates.

currentProjects.title: SPIIR pipeline analysis
currentProjects.description: This project studies the SPIIR low-latency compact-binary pipeline from the online command layer through matched filtering, GPU multirate SPIIR filtering, CUDA postcoh calculation, coherent SNR, null SNR, detector chi-square, network chi-square, background accumulation, rank/FAP construction, FAR assignment, and final candidate selection.
currentProjects.role: I trace source-code paths, build formula-level explanations, separate raw features from ranking statistics, and evaluate how the existing coherent pipeline could be extended toward a new single-detector likelihood-ratio/rank/FAR branch.
currentProjects.progress: The core online/postcoh path has been mapped locally and on the server. The single-detector notes now derive the chain from local complex-SNR windows to (rho, chi-square), log likelihood ratio, rank, empirical background tail, and FAR. The current status is analysis/design rather than production code: the necessary input fields exist, but coordinated changes are still needed in postcoh emission, background statistics, FAR assignment, FinalSink selection, and launch wiring.

currentProjects.title: DWD foreground-noise injection for space-based detectors
currentProjects.description: This project injects Galactic double-white-dwarf and Galactic-binary foregrounds into LISA/Taiji-style detector simulations. The key principle is to sum sources after detector response projection, not at the source-frame polarization level, because every binary has its own sky location, polarization, inclination, orbital modulation, and light-time delay.
currentProjects.role: I designed and documented the response-first workflow, SNR triage strategy, grouped GPU cache layout, resolved/unresolved split, and final TDI A/E/T production plan.
currentProjects.progress: A 29-million-source Sangria/Taiji TDI2 SNR production run completed on the server. It used 360 days of data, 36 ten-day segments, 0.4 Hz sampling, 30 workers, a threshold SNR of 10, and selected 17,448 high-SNR sources. The current next step is to move from SNR selection and ASD diagnostics to explicit large-catalog foreground injection using summed six-link response caches and a single final TDI pass.

currentProjects.title: LIGO calibration uncertainty in stochastic-background analysis
currentProjects.description: This project studies how calibration uncertainty enters isotropic stochastic gravitational-wave background analysis. It compares late post-processing corrections to strain-level calibration injection, investigates frequency-independent and frequency-dependent calibration models, checks insertion-point effects, and tests explicit calibration nuisance parameters in Bayesian inference.
currentProjects.role: I reconstructed the codebase into a paper-style scientific narrative, extracted notebook figures, organized the result logic, and connected estimator-level shifts to physical calibration parameters and Bayesian posterior broadening.
currentProjects.progress: The local English manuscript and calibration report are complete drafts. They cover three injected SGWB spectral shapes, six calibration families, post-processing versus strain-level injection, final-add/before-delta-sigma-cut/on-spectrogram insertion points, five physical calibration parameters, and two-parameter versus seven-parameter PE. The remaining work is to clean known implementation caveats and condense the report into a submission-ready manuscript.

currentProjects.title: Triplet scalar model: collider and gravitational-wave constraints
currentProjects.description: This project connects a triplet scalar model to gravitational-wave and collider observables. It combines HDECAY-based decay information, thermodynamic phase-transition scans, gravitational-wave spectral mapping, interpolation over (mSigma, a2), and Bilby/dynesty parameter estimation using detector and astrophysical nuisance parameters.
currentProjects.role: I work on scan monitoring, invalid-region repair, interpolation validation, 6D and 10D Bayesian inference drivers, GPU execution, checkpoint verification, and the interpretation of gravitational-wave-selected scalar-sector regions.
currentProjects.progress: The current server-side scan is actively running. The live snapshot on 2026-05-01 reported 521,030 completed rows out of 574,839 known rows, 490,077 valid rows, 354 timeouts, 50 active jobs, and current mass around 111.75 GeV. The live relative-SNR watcher reported 521,029 processed rows, 378,324 positive-SNR points, and maximum relative SNR about 188.6.

futureDirections.title: Turn detector simulations into publication-grade comparisons
futureDirections.description: Finalize the time-domain versus frequency-domain comparison, document channel-covariance assumptions, and keep all figure/table provenance reproducible.

futureDirections.title: Bridge foreground simulation and stochastic-background inference
futureDirections.description: Use DWD foreground products not only as diagnostic plots, but as realistic nuisance structure in downstream LISA/Taiji inference.

futureDirections.title: Connect gravitational-wave measurements to particle physics
futureDirections.description: Develop cleaner maps from SGWB posterior regions to xSM, CxSM, and triplet-scalar parameter spaces, including collider-facing Higgs observables.
